component: URL-loading query runners (json/csv/excel/url) and HTTP data sources

loops.Q1_Coverage_Gap: None. All entry points listed in attack-surface-map.md have at least one VALIDATED or NEEDS-DEEPER hypothesis.
Q2_Chain_Seeding: PH-01/PH-02/PH-04 (SSRF via JSON/URL runners) can chain into cloud credential theft or internal service access.
PH-03 (CSV/Excel redirect SSRF) can chain into data exfiltration or access to internal admin APIs.
PH-06 (Elasticsearch system indices) can chain into credential/role disclosure enabling broader cluster compromise.
PH-07 (Drill filesystem access) can chain into host secret exposure and escalation.
loops.Q3_Fragile_Safety: None. No INVALIDATED hypotheses with Fragile fragility score in evidence.
loops.Q4_Model_Coverage: All entry points were addressed by backward/contradiction/causal rounds. Trust chain gaps were covered by PH-01/02/03/04.
Q5_Impact_Multiplication: PH-05 (Round 3 NEEDS-DEEPER) — if non-admins can set base_url, SSRF exposure expands beyond admin-only misconfiguration, increasing severity and likelihood across JSON runner use.
